title: Change a Light Bulb
summary: Safely replace a burned-out light bulb with the correct type and wattage for the fixture.
learnerPromise: Replace a burned-out light bulb safely and choose the right type.
whyItMatters: A burned-out bulb makes it hard to see and can be unsafe. Knowing how to replace one keeps your space well-lit and comfortable.
realLifeUses: Replacing a bulb in a bedroom lamp
Fixing a hallway light that went out
Swapping a kitchen bulb for a brighter one
Helping change a bulb in a hard-to-reach spot with a sturdy step stool
youWillLearn: How to turn off power before changing a bulb
How to match the bulb base type and wattage
How to remove the old bulb and install the new one safely
What to do with the old bulb
miniChallenge: Replace one light bulb in your home, matching the wattage and base type.
steps: Turn off the light switch and let the old bulb cool if it was just on.
Check the old bulb for the wattage and base type printed on it.
Get a new bulb that matches the wattage and base type.
Gently turn the old bulb counterclockwise to remove it.
Insert the new bulb and turn it clockwise until it is snug. Turn the switch on to test.
completionCriteria: You matched the wattage and base type of the new bulb
You installed the new bulb without forcing it
The light turns on and works properly
commonProblems: The new bulb does not light up — try it in a different socket to test
The bulb breaks while removing it — turn off power and use gloves to remove the base
You are not sure of the wattage — check the fixture label or the old bulb carefully
tips: Use a sturdy step stool if the fixture is out of reach
LED bulbs use less energy and last longer than traditional ones
Never force a bulb — if it does not fit, double-check the base type
